title: ranunculus cymbalaria var. typicus
intro: Ranunculus cymbalaria var. typicus, commonly known as the 'alpine buttercup', is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral compounds. The health benefits of Ranunculus cymbalaria var. typicus include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in tea infusions or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although it is essential to exercise caution when handling the plant due to its toxic nature.
